places.vegan_detail: No declared vegan menu. Warabi mochi (bracken-starch jelly) and most cold drinks are likely vegan by ingredients, but there are no allergen declarations. Almond toast uses butter; pancakes likely contain dairy and egg. The Yamazaki convenience shop section sells onigiri and sandwiches — ingredient labeling on packaged items may be checked in-store.
places.neighborhood: 本町・姫路城入口 (Honcho, Himeji Castle entrance)
places.notes: 大手門茶屋 is a public rest house operated by the 一般財団法人姫路市まちづくり振興機構 (Himeji City Urban Development Foundation), a civic body that manages tourism and urban facilities around the World Heritage castle grounds. It sits inside the Otemon (main gate) parking lot, directly facing Himeji C

chef_bio.background: 大手門茶屋 is a public rest house operated by the 一般財団法人姫路市まちづくり振興機構 (Himeji City Urban Development Foundation), a civic body that manages tourism and urban facilities around the World Heritage castle grounds. It sits inside the Otemon (main gate) parking lot, directly facing Himeji Castle, and has served as the primary refreshment stop for castle visitors for many years.
chef_bio.philosophy: A city-run rest house prioritizing accessibility and convenience for castle tourists, offering light refreshments, original Himeji Castle merchandise, and free wheelchair rentals to welcome visitors of all abilities.
chef_bio.specialty: Almond toast — a slice of thick-cut bread spread with butter and topped with chopped almonds, considered a Himeji local specialty — alongside soft-serve ice cream and a range of Japanese-style lattes (matcha, caramel, chocolate).

anecdotes.text: The shop was temporarily closed during a typhoon in August 2024 but resumed normal operation thereafter, reflecting its role as a managed public facility rather than a private business.

cultural_comfort.note: A tourist-facing rest house at the Himeji Castle entrance serving an international visitor audience; signage and service are accessible to non-Japanese speakers.

places.name: 姫山茶寮 (Himeyama Saryo)
places.gf_detail: Kaiseki cuisine has many simmered dishes and sauces using soy sauce (contains wheat), and battered dishes such as tempura are also served (there is a note of "with salt only," but not for all items). No gluten-free accommodation is indicated. Presence of a dedicated fryer is unknown. Those with wheat allergy or celiac disease must inquire directly when reserving. Assessment: not celiac-safe.
places.vegan_detail: The standard kaiseki course includes sashimi, grilled fish, beef dishes, and more, and plant-based-only accommodation has not been confirmed. The dashi is presumed bonito-flake/kombu-based but not officially stated. Because there is a note of "consult us for a healthy menu," inquiry is advised. No vegan accommodation is indicated. Assessment: not reliably vegan.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Tue, Thu–Sun & holidays: lunch 11:30–14:00 (LO 13:00), dinner 17:00–21:30 (LO 20:00). Closed Wed, plus irregular days off.
places.neighborhood: Northwest of Himeji Castle, Korikimachi
places.notes: The owner discovered a building converted from a traditional house over 100 years old (said to be a former sake brewery), fell in love with the charm of the space, and opened the restaurant. The building stands along the wall of Himeji Castle, in a quiet corner northwest of the castle.

chef_bio.origin: 兵庫県姫路市小利木町
chef_bio.background: 築100年以上の古民家（元酒蔵とも伝わる）を改装した建物を発見したオーナーが、その空間の魅力に惚れ込み店を開いた。 建物は姫路城の城壁沿い、姫路城北西の閑静な一角に佇む。
chef_bio.philosophy: 「自分がおいしいと思うものをお客様にも楽しんでいただけたら」——既製品を一切使わず、近隣の信頼する鮮魚店・青果店から仕入れた旬の素材を最大限に活かす手間ひまを惜しまない料理作り。
chef_bio.specialty: 大名つづら（10種以上の季節料理＋抹茶フォンデュのセット御膳）および抹茶フォンデュ

anecdotes.text: 署名デザートの抹茶フォンデュのソースは、オーナーの奥様が試行錯誤を重ねて完成させたもの。SNSで話題となり学生から大人まで幅広い層に人気が広がった。
店内には水引アートや繊細なバラン細工が飾られており、そのうちの一つは姫路城を模して作られている。オーナーが技術を磨いた手仕事の片鱗が随所に見られる。
看板メニュー「大名つづら」の中身は仕入れた食材によって毎日変わる。胡麻豆腐は手作りで、そのままでもたれ不要と評判。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 天ぷら・油物メニューあり（「塩のみで」との記述はあるが専用フライヤーの使用有無は未確認）。小麦を含む揚げ物と同一厨房・フライヤーを共有している可能性がある。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 懐石料理の標準的な調理で醤油（小麦含有）が煮物・タレ・だし等に使用されると推定される。グルテンフリー醤油（タマリ等）使用の明示なし。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 刺身・牛肉・焼き魚・海老天ぷらなど動物性食材を同一厨房で扱うため、植物性料理との接触リスクがある。ビーガン専用調理設備の記述なし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 「ヘルシーメニューご相談」との表記あり（ぐるなびページ）。完全予約制のため、予約時に食物アレルギー・食事制限を事前相談できる環境にある。ただしアレルギー対応の専門的な体制や認証の記載はなし。

positives.text: 完全予約制で少人数制のため、事前に食の制限を伝える余地がある。オーナーが素材を個別に選んでいる小規模独立店であり、融通が利きやすい可能性がある。

cultural_comfort.note: 完全予約制の懐石料理店。英語メニューあり（訪日外国人向け対応）。靴を脱いで入店する畳座敷あり。カード決済可能で外国人対応の準備はあるが、基本的に日本語のやり取りが中心。

places.name: ラーメンマサマサ (Ramen Masamasa)
places.gf_detail: Wheat is central to every dish: house-made wheat noodles are the signature, and the shoyu tare is made with conventional wheat-containing soy sauce. No gluten-free noodle or tamari alternative is mentioned anywhere. Cross-contamination from noodle production in-house is near-certain.
places.vegan_detail: All menu items contain meat or fish. The shoyu ramen uses chicken-based broth with chicken oil; the niboshi ramen is built on dried sardine dashi; mazesoba features braised pork; even the tsukemen dipping broth is animal-based. No plant-based alternatives are offered.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Sun 11:00–14:30, closed Mon
places.neighborhood: 豊沢町 (South of Himeji Station)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Owner Hiramatsu was born in Kanagawa but grew up in Himeji, which he considers his hometown. He spent 16 years as a corporate manager before making the leap to ramen — a decision that required six months of persuasion with his family. He opened the original shop, 麺昌まさ (Menshow Ma

chef_bio.origin: Himeji, Hyogo Prefecture (relocated from earlier Himeji location)
chef_bio.background: Owner Hiramatsu was born in Kanagawa but grew up in Himeji, which he considers his hometown. He spent 16 years as a corporate manager before making the leap to ramen — a decision that required six months of persuasion with his family. He opened the original shop, 麺昌まさ (Menshow Masa), on November 4, 2015, and relocated and rebranded it as Ramen Masamasa on December 23, 2023.
chef_bio.philosophy: Hiramatsu describes his approach as seeking ramen 'where diners recall the ingredients and producers. ' He favors clear, delicate dashi over milky broths and continuously refines his recipes each year, visiting other acclaimed shops and building within the ramen community. He uses specialty flour, premium hand-selected soy sauce, jidori local chicken stock, and niboshi (dried sardine) as a primary aromatic.
chef_bio.specialty: Shoyu ramen with aromatic chicken oil and deeply layered soy-sauce broth; award-winning kombu-water tsukemen

anecdotes.text: Hiramatsu says he is 'someone who obsesses over details and constantly seeks knowledge' — a self-description that echoes his 16-year corporate career as a manager before pivoting entirely to ramen.

The kombu-water tsukemen won the Gold Prize in the tsukemen category at the 2023 Ultimate Ramen Kinki edition, and the shoyu ramen earned Runner-Up Grand Prix in Pia's Ultimate Ramen Kansai 2025 edition.

gf_cross_contamination.text: House-made wheat noodles are produced on-site; wheat flour is in active use throughout service. Cross-contamination for anyone with celiac disease or serious wheat allergy is effectively unavoidable.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The shoyu tare uses conventional wheat-containing soy sauce selected by the owner for its aroma profile. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Chicken oil (鶏油) is used in the shoyu ramen broth, niboshi (dried sardine) is a core aromatic in the niboshi ramen, and braised pork features in the mazesoba. Animal products are present in every menu item with no plant-based alternative available.

positives.text: The owner's background as a detail-obsessed craftsman and his practice of hand-selecting premium ingredients suggest consistent, careful preparation — though no specific allergy protocol has been documented.

cultural_comfort.note: Counter-heavy lunch-only ramen shop with no English signage or menus noted; ordering is straightforward at the counter but expect Japanese only.

places.name: おでんと串カツ姫路のお店 (Oden to Kushikatsu Himeji no Omise)
places.gf_detail: Kushikatsu (the restaurant's core offering alongside oden) are deep-fried in wheat-based batter — wheat is central to the menu. The oden broth is made from konbu and katsuo (bonito) dashi. Standard Japanese soy sauce (which contains wheat) is used in the ginger soy sauce condiment. No gluten-free options, dedicated fryer, or allergen accommodation information appears anywhere on the restaurant's own pages or third-party listings. Celiac diners should avoid.
places.vegan_detail: The oden broth is explicitly described as konbu and katsuo (bonito fish) dashi. Kushikatsu typically includes pork and other meats. The menu features sashimi, grilled chicken (hinepon), beef tendon, and egg dishes. No plant-based or vegan options are indicated anywhere on the menu or in any source. Hidden animal-derived dashi is present throughout.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–23:15 daily (food L.O. 22:30, drinks L.O. 22:45); closed January 1 only
places.neighborhood: Himeji Noren-gai / 1 min walk from Himeji Stn
places.notes: Operated by 株式会社otto, this restaurant occupies a stall inside 姫路のれん街 (Himeji Noren Street), a retro-style covered arcade of izakaya-format eateries on the ground floor of the Shin Festa Building, a one-minute walk from Himeji Station. The noren-gai concept evokes the atmosphere o

chef_bio.background: Operated by 株式会社otto, this restaurant occupies a stall inside 姫路のれん街 (Himeji Noren Street), a retro-style covered arcade of izakaya-format eateries on the ground floor of the Shin Festa Building, a one-minute walk from Himeji Station. The noren-gai concept evokes the atmosphere of a Showa-era alley of red-lantern drinking establishments.
chef_bio.specialty: Himeji oden — slow-simmered since early morning in konbu and katsuo dashi, served with the city's signature house-made ginger soy sauce dipping condiment. The restaurant also serves kushikatsu (deep-fried skewers) from ¥132, including novelty items such as konnyaku shaped like Himeji Castle.

anecdotes.text: The konnyaku (devil's tongue jelly) in the oden set is molded in the shape of Himeji Castle, a whimsical nod to the city's most famous landmark.

The restaurant has been featured on TV and regional media programs highlighting Himeji local cuisine including chanpon-yaki, hineppon, and bokkake (beef offal simmered with konnyaku in sweet-spicy sauce).

gf_cross_contamination.text: Kushikatsu (deep-fried battered skewers) is one of the two core menu categories. Wheat batter is in heavy use throughout service. No dedicated gluten-free fryer is mentioned anywhere.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The ginger soy sauce condiment (生姜醤油) served with oden is standard Japanese shoyu, which contains wheat. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The oden broth is made from konbu and katsuo (bonito fish) dashi. All oden ingredients simmer in this shared broth, making plant-based isolation impossible even for nominally vegetable items.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling protocols, allergen menus, or dietary accommodation procedures are published on any platform. The restaurant's own site, Tabelog, HotPepper, Gurunavi, and Hitosara all lack allergen information.

cultural_comfort.note: Casual Showa-era izakaya atmosphere inside Himeji Noren Street; staff are noted as bright and welcoming but menus and service are in Japanese with no confirmed English-language support.

places.name: 魚町まつびし (Uomachi Matsubishi)
places.gf_detail: Standard Japanese kaiseki relies heavily on wheat-based soy sauce (koikuchi shoyu) in sauces, marinades, and dipping preparations, and dashi stocks may be combined with soy sauce. Fried dishes (e.g. scorpionfish karaage noted in reviews) use wheat batter. No dedicated gluten-free menu, no tamari substitution, and no allergen information is published. Celiac diners should call ahead; safe options cannot be assumed without direct confirmation from the kitchen.
places.vegan_detail: This is a traditional kaiseki and kappo restaurant built around Seto Inland Sea seafood — dishes regularly include katsuo/bonito dashi, fish-based broths, shellfish, whale hot pot, and sashimi. There is no stated vegan menu and no indication that plant-based courses are available. A diner with vegan requirements should contact the restaurant well in advance to discuss options, though a fully vegan kaiseki course would be very difficult here.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat, pre-holidays & post-holidays 17:30–22:00 (L.O. 21:30), closed Sun & holidays
places.neighborhood: 魚町 (Uomachi), near Sanyo Himeji Station
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] An established kappo and kaiseki restaurant in Himeji's Uomachi district, in operation for multiple decades based on its multi-floor building and retro-era décor described by visitors as evocative of Japan's bubble period. The ground floor features counter seating; an upper floor

chef_bio.background: An established kappo and kaiseki restaurant in Himeji's Uomachi district, in operation for multiple decades based on its multi-floor building and retro-era décor described by visitors as evocative of Japan's bubble period. The ground floor features counter seating; an upper floor houses a whisky bar.
chef_bio.philosophy: A commitment to showcasing the natural flavors of carefully sourced local fish; the menu changes with the seasons and emphasizes the depth of ingredient quality over elaborate technique.
chef_bio.specialty: Seasonal Seto Inland Sea fish and shellfish — over 100 small-plate (ippin) dishes anchored by premium seafood; winter specialties include fugu sashimi (tessa) and nabe courses featuring oysters, conger eel, duck, and seasonal seafood.

anecdotes.text: A visiting food blogger noted that the rice porridge (zosui) at the close of the hot-pot course was finished with nori and chives rather than the more common green onion — a small but memorable personal touch.
The same blogger observed that fugu, normally a signature item, was unavailable on their visit due to rising market prices — illustrating how closely the menu tracks seasonal and market conditions.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No published information on cross-contamination controls. Fried items (scorpionfish karaage) appear on menus, indicating shared frying oil with wheat-battered foods is possible.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Traditional kappo cooking uses wheat-containing soy sauce throughout — in dipping sauces, simmered dishes, and marinades. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere in available sources.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All courses are built around fish and seafood; bonito/katsuo dashi is standard in Japanese kappo cooking. No vegan-safe preparation lines are indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information on allergy handling procedures is publicly available. Diners with celiac disease or serious allergies should call the restaurant directly (079-288-0878) well before visiting.

cultural_comfort.note: Traditional upscale kappo setting aimed at business entertaining; staff dress formally and the atmosphere is refined — some comfort with Japanese dining customs is helpful, though no English-language service is indicated.

places.name: おばんざいと地酒 ゆう菜 (Obanzai to Jizake Yusai)
places.gf_detail: Across obanzai and washoku in general, soy sauce (contains wheat), mirin, and dashi-shoyu are routinely used. Items using wheat flour, such as spring rolls, have been confirmed. No gluten-free accommodation is indicated. Presence of a dedicated fryer is unknown. Celiacs must confirm details by phone before visiting. Assessment: not celiac-safe.
places.vegan_detail: The daily obanzai centers on seasonal vegetables and offers a solid range of vegetable dishes, but items using bonito dashi, chicken, and seafood are mixed in. Animal-based items such as "Himeji oden," "sake-steamed clams," and "fresh sashimi" are part of regular service. No plant-based-only course or explicit vegan menu could be confirmed. Confirm before visiting. Assessment: not reliably vegan.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Fri 17:00–23:00 (LO 22:30), Sat, Sun & holidays 15:00–23:00 (LO 22:30). Note: on Sat, Sun & holidays 15:00–17:00 only obanzai is served. Closed Mon (or the following Tue if Mon is a holiday).
places.neighborhood: Kubomachi, around Sanyo Himeji Stn
places.cuisine: Obanzai / local sake / Kyoto-style small dishes
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Opened September 1, 2025, in Kubomachi, Himeji. A small dining spot on the 1st floor of the MISTRAL Kubomachi Building, a 5-minute walk from Sanyo Himeji Station.

chef_bio.background: 2025年9月1日に姫路市久保町にオープン。 山陽姫路駅から徒歩5分、MISTRAL久保町ビル1階に構える小さな小料理屋。
chef_bio.philosophy: 「美味しく、楽しく、健康に。 」をコンセプトに、管理栄養士の資格を持つ女将が栄養を考えた日替わりおばんざいと一品料理を提供。 地元・播磨の地酒を常時10種類以上厳選して揃える。
chef_bio.specialty: 日替わりおばんざい（季節野菜の家庭料理）と播磨の地酒

anecdotes.text: オーナーは管理栄養士の資格保有者で、健康面を考慮しながら季節の食材を使った日替わりメニューを毎日手作りしている。
ブログレポートによると、開店直後から常連客が多く訪れ、予約が取りにくい状況が続いているとの記述があった。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 春巻きなど小麦粉使用メニューが通常ラインナップに含まれており、調理場での交差汚染リスクを排除できない。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 和食・おばんざいの調理では一般的な醤油（小麦含有）が使用されると考えられる。グルテンフリー醤油使用の明示はない。

vegan_cross_contact.text: かつお出汁・魚介・鶏肉を使うメニューが同じ厨房で調理される。植物性のみへの対応について明記なし。

staff_allergy_handling.text: オーナーが管理栄養士の資格を持つため、アレルギーへの理解は期待できるが、公式にアレルギー対応を謳った記述は確認されていない。来店前に電話（070-2352-3200）での事前確認を推奨。

positives.text: オーナーは管理栄養士の資格保持者で、健康を意識した季節野菜中心のおばんざいを提供しており、野菜メニューの種類が多い。

cultural_comfort.note: 小さなカウンター中心の小料理屋。英語メニューや英語対応スタッフの情報なし。日本語でのやり取りが基本。

places.name: 喫茶 フクナガ (Kissa Fukunaga)
places.gf_detail: No gluten-free options or allergy information documented anywhere across multiple sources. The signature black beef curry (developed with 30+ spices) likely contains wheat-based soy sauce; pasta is a core menu item; canelé contains wheat flour. Wheat is pervasive throughout the kitchen. No dedicated fryer information available. Celiacs should ask before ordering and be prepared that safe options may be very limited.
places.vegan_detail: No vegan-specific options documented. The menu centres on beef curry (3-day braised brisket), egg sandwiches with dashi-rolled egg, pasta, and dairy-heavy desserts (Italian pudding, canelé, cream sodas). Hidden dashi and dairy are very likely throughout. Visitors should ask staff, but vegan options appear very limited if available at all.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–21:00 (L.O. 20:00), irregular days off
places.neighborhood: Minamimachi (1 min walk from Sanyo-Himeji Stn, 3 min walk from JR Himeji Stn north exit)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] Opened on April 15, 2022 in the Kamihata Building directly adjacent to the Bon Marché Himeji shopping arcade, one minute on foot from Sanyo-Himeji Station. The café is operated by the Usagi Group (食菜家うさぎ), a Himeji-based dining company that runs several izakaya-style restaurants

chef_bio.background: Opened on April 15, 2022 in the Kamihata Building directly adjacent to the Bon Marché Himeji shopping arcade, one minute on foot from Sanyo-Himeji Station. The café is operated by the Usagi Group (食菜家うさぎ), a Himeji-based dining company that runs several izakaya-style restaurants in the area.
chef_bio.philosophy: The Usagi Group owner personally researched curries across Japan before collaborating with the kitchen team to develop the menu, aiming to pair serious, craft-level cooking with the relaxed, nostalgic atmosphere of a classic kissaten.
chef_bio.specialty: 自家製黒ビーフカレー (homemade black beef curry) — developed over 18 months using 30+ spices, with the beef braised for three days; colourful cream sodas (five varieties, with the Fukunaga Green selling approximately 70 cups per day) are also a signature draw.

anecdotes.text: The signature black beef curry took 18 months to perfect, with the owner travelling the country studying curry before landing on a recipe using over 30 spices and a three-day braising process for the brisket.
The Fukunaga Green cream soda — a melon-and-vanilla soft drink with a striking emerald colour — became an instant hit, reportedly selling around 70 cups per day after opening.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No allergen or soy sauce information published. The curry, pasta, and egg sandwich are all likely to involve wheat-based soy sauce or wheat-containing ingredients; no source confirms tamari or GF substitutes are available.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy and eggs are core ingredients across the menu (Italian pudding, canelé, egg sandwich, cream sodas). No vegan cross-contact protocols documented.

cultural_comfort.note: Retro kissaten atmosphere with jazz; menus and signage are in Japanese only, but the relaxed café setting and solo-diner-friendly layout make it approachable for non-Japanese visitors who can point to menu items.

places.name: アプリーレ (Aprire)
places.gf_detail: Standard Italian restaurant serving pizza and pasta, both of which are wheat-based. No gluten-free pasta or pizza alternatives are mentioned on any source. Bread (baguette) is a standard starter. No dedicated fryer information available. Wheat cross-contamination is a realistic concern in any standard Italian kitchen. Celiacs should call ahead (079-222-5511) to ask about gluten-free accommodation; currently no evidence supports safe dining for celiac without direct confirmation.
places.vegan_detail: Standard Italian menu centered on meat, fish, pasta, and pizza. No vegan or vegetarian labelling found on any source. Dishes include prosciutto, seafood pasta (sea urchin cream, carpaccio), and egg-topped preparations. Dairy (butter, cream, cheese) is prevalent throughout. Vegans should call ahead — Italian technique here relies heavily on animal products and hidden ingredients such as parsley-basil butter on bread service and cream-based sauces.
places.neighborhood: 十二所前町 (Jusshoshomacho)
places.notes: Aprire is a multi-floor Italian restaurant tucked into a quiet residential-commercial block in Himeji's Jusshoshomacho district, a short walk south of Himeji Castle and roughly five minutes from Sanyo-Himeji Station. Despite its unassuming, somewhat hidden location on a low-traff

chef_bio.background: Aprire is a multi-floor Italian restaurant tucked into a quiet residential-commercial block in Himeji's Jusshoshomacho district, a short walk south of Himeji Castle and roughly five minutes from Sanyo-Himeji Station. Despite its unassuming, somewhat hidden location on a low-traffic street, it has built a loyal following of regulars over the years. The restaurant runs on a lean team — reportedly one owner and one chef — keeping the atmosphere personal and consistent.
chef_bio.philosophy: No explicit philosophy statement found; the restaurant's reputation rests on seasonal ingredients, consistent quality, and attentive hospitality — staff have been noted holding food for returning guests as a gesture of care.
chef_bio.specialty: Seasonal Italian cuisine including seafood pasta (notably sea urchin tomato cream), thin-crust pizza, carpaccio, and risotto; the house-made basil butter served with baguette is a recurring standout in guest accounts.

anecdotes.text: A blogger noted that despite minimal foot traffic on weekdays in the Tatsumachi area, Aprire draws a steady crowd of regulars — 'a comfortable, welcoming establishment' even on quiet evenings.
One guest recounted that staff set aside the remainder of their basil butter dish for them to enjoy on a return visit the following day, illustrating the restaurant's attentiveness.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free preparation area or protocols mentioned in any source; standard Italian kitchen with pasta, pizza, and bread service means wheat cross-contamination is a realistic concern.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce identified in sourced menu descriptions; cuisine is Italian, but wheat is present throughout via pasta, pizza dough, and bread. No gluten-free alternatives noted anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy (butter, cream, cheese) and meat products are core to the menu. Basil butter is served as a standard bread accompaniment. No vegan-safe preparation protocols mentioned.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling protocols or allergen menu documented in any fetched source. Direct phone contact (079-222-5511) is the only confirmed path to obtain allergy accommodation information.

positives.text: Small independent restaurant with a close-knit team (reportedly one owner, one chef), which may allow flexible accommodation requests if arranged in advance by phone.

cultural_comfort.note: A long-established local Italian spot with a loyal regular crowd; staff are attentive and hospitable, but the menu and communication are in Japanese with no English-language materials noted.

places.name: からあげ 秀 (Karaage Shu)
places.gf_detail: Standard Japanese karaage batter contains wheat flour, and the marinade almost certainly uses conventional soy sauce (which contains wheat). No gluten-free options are listed anywhere online, no dedicated fryer information is available, and the shop has published no allergen information. Anyone with celiac disease should not assume safety without directly confirming ingredients and fryer practices with staff before ordering.
places.vegan_detail: This is a dedicated chicken karaage shop — every menu item is meat-based (fried chicken, croquettes, fried takoyaki). There are no vegan options and no plant-based dishes on the menu.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Thu 11:00–17:00, Fri–Sun 11:00–17:30, closed Mon
places.neighborhood: Honmachi 68, Ino Yashiki (along Otemae-dori toward the castle, Karo Yashiki area)
places.notes: [Mom & Pop] からあげ 秀 opened in March 2023 inside the いの屋敷 complex (also known as 家老屋敷A棟) at Himeji's Honmachi 68 — a historic-district shopfront at the entrance to the Karoyashiki ruins park on the main approach road to Himeji Castle.

chef_bio.background: からあげ 秀 opened in March 2023 inside the いの屋敷 complex (also known as 家老屋敷A棟) at Himeji's Honmachi 68 — a historic-district shopfront at the entrance to the Karoyashiki ruins park on the main approach road to Himeji Castle.
chef_bio.specialty: 国産からあげ — freshly fried domestic chicken (Banshu-raised free-range bird) in a mixed breast-and-thigh portion, served in approx. five minutes from order. A signature side attraction is the まるごとオレンジジュース, fresh juice served inside a hollowed-out whole orange.

anecdotes.text: Reviewers describe it as a hidden-gem rest stop for castle visitors, valued as much for its whole-orange juice as its fried chicken. An optional 本気スパイス (serious spice blend) can be requested for heat.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information is available about fryer sharing between different menu items (chicken, croquettes, fried takoyaki). Cross-contamination risk cannot be ruled out.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The karaage marinade almost certainly uses conventional Japanese soy sauce, which contains wheat. The batter is standard wheat-flour coating. Neither the shop nor any third-party source has published allergen or ingredient details.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire menu is meat-based; there is no plant-based cooking context here and cross-contact is not a relevant concern given no vegan items exist.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information on staff allergy awareness or handling procedures is available from any source reviewed. Direct contact is required: 080-9014-9354.

cultural_comfort.note: A small local stall near Himeji Castle aimed primarily at Japanese tourists and locals; English menus or English-speaking staff are not confirmed, but simple counter ordering with pointing at menu boards is manageable.

places.name: たけだの穴子めし まねき本店 (Takeda no Anago-meshi Maneki Honten)
places.gf_detail: The tare (sauce) and cooking liquid are made with standard Japanese soy sauce, which contains wheat. The rice is cooked in anago dashi infused with that same sauce. No allergen disclosure is published and no GF claim is made. Celiac diners should not assume safety and would need to call ahead (079-223-8652) to ask about the exact shoyu used — though substitution is unlikely for a bento format.
places.vegan_detail: The entire menu is anago (conger eel) — there are no plant-based options. The rice is cooked in anago-based dashi stock. Nothing here is vegan.
places.hours_raw: 09:00–20:00 daily; irregular closures follow Festa South Building schedule
places.neighborhood: 駅前町 — Himeji Station north exit, inside Festa South Building
places.notes: Maneki Foods (まねき食品) has been a Himeji institution since 1888, when they began selling anago (conger eel) sushi at Himeji Station. The company became famous for ekisoba — the station noodle combining Chinese noodles with Japanese-style dashi — now considered Himeji's soul food. A

chef_bio.background: Maneki Foods (まねき食品) has been a Himeji institution since 1888, when they began selling anago (conger eel) sushi at Himeji Station. The company became famous for ekisoba — the station noodle combining Chinese noodles with Japanese-style dashi — now considered Himeji's soul food. After more than 130 years and six generations, the 6th-generation president spent one and a half years developing a bento that would honor that original anago sushi tradition in a new format.
chef_bio.philosophy: The shop aims to make anago-meshi a proud souvenir of Himeji — something that can only be tasted here, prepared at peak freshness for each order.
chef_bio.specialty: Anago bento: thick live-caught conger eel white-grilled then seared with a proprietary sauce, served over Hyogo-grown Kinumusume rice cooked in anago dashi, finished with rare domestic Kinbanshima sesame and Kishu grape sansho pepper

anecdotes.text: The 6th-generation president spent a year and a half developing the recipe before opening in August 2020, wanting to surpass the quality of typical station bento and reach the level of their celebrated anago sushi.
The rice is cooked in a custom iron pot using dashi extracted from the anago itself — an unusual technique that concentrates eel flavor into each grain.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No allergen disclosure is published. The cooking liquid and sauce contain standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat). Cross-contamination risk within the bento kitchen is unknown.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The proprietary tare used to glaze and simmer the anago is made with soy sauce; Japanese soy sauce (koikuchi) standardly contains wheat. The rice is cooked in the same anago-shoyu dashi. No GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The entire product is animal-based (eel, eel dashi, shoyu). There is no plant-based item on the menu and no segregation relevant to vegan contact.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No published allergy protocol. Reservations are available and phone inquiries are possible (079-223-8652), but no allergy accommodation policy has been documented.

positives.text: Simple, focused menu of just two bento sizes means ingredient consistency is high — what you get today is what every other customer gets.

cultural_comfort.note: Counter takeout window inside a modern shopping building; ordering is straightforward but staff interaction is in Japanese.

places.name: ametuchi COFFEE & ORGANIC BAKED (アメツチ コーヒー＆オーガニックベイクド)
places.gf_detail: Organic cafe run by an athlete couple. Specific rice-flour (米粉) items are gluten-free: the 有機米粉ワッフル (organic rice-flour waffle) and the 米粉キャロットケーキ, which is described on-site as グルテンフリー. The shop states it wants to offer 『小麦アレルギーの人も楽しめるメニューを』 (menus that people with wheat allergy can also enjoy). CROSS-CONTACT CAVEAT: the same kitchen bakes homemade rye bread using organic wheat 『春よ恋』(Haru-yo-koi) blended with rye — wheat flour is handled on-premises, so this is a shared kitchen, not a celiac-dedicated one. Order only the named rice-flour items and tell staff it is a coeliac child.
places.vegan_detail: Organic, additive-free focus but not a vegan kitchen — many items use egg, cheese and butter (egg-cheese toast, tomato-cheese toast, red-bean butter toast, cakes). A few plant-based items exist but vegan choices are limited; confirm each item.
places.hours_raw: 9:00–17:00 (L.O. 16:30), morning menu until 11:30. For closing days, refer to the calendar on the official Instagram/Highlights.
places.neighborhood: 飾磨区加茂 (Shikama-ku Kamo), 姫路市 Himeji
places.notes: [GF] Only the named rice-flour items (米粉ワッフル、米粉キャロットケーキ) are gluten-free. The bakery makes wheat/rye bread daily in the same kitchen, so flour is airborne — treat as options with real cross-contact risk. Tell staff it is coeliac (セリアック) and stick to the labelled 米粉/グルテンフリー items.

chef_bio.background: ametuchi is run by an athlete couple; one holds an international football (soccer) referee credential and has officiated FIFA-level matches. They cite childhood illness as the reason for prioritising food-based health, choosing organic and additive-free ingredients sourced from across Japan. The shop relocated to Shikama-ku Kamo (from Ōtsu-ku Tenma) and reopened there on 12 January 2025. Coffee training was done under the mentor roaster 『JOKI COFFEE』 in Kōchi, whose five specialty beans they serve.
chef_bio.philosophy: 『オーガニックをより身近に』 — make organic everyday and approachable, additive-free, including menus wheat-allergy customers can enjoy.
chef_bio.specialty: Specialty coffee, organic morning plates, rice-flour (米粉) waffles and cakes.

anecdotes.text: Homemade almond butter is made with Kikaijima cane sugar and Yotsuba butter; European vintage dishware (Denmark Kronjørn, Finland Arabia) is used.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: TANOSU取材記事とてくてくひめじ、公式サイトより。アスリート夫婦が営むオーガニックカフェで、2025年1月に飾磨区加茂へ移転オープン。米粉ワッフル・米粉キャロットケーキ(グルテンフリー)を提供する一方、有機小麦『春よ恋』＋ライ麦の自家製パンも焼く。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 自家製ライ麦パンは有機小麦『春よ恋』＋有機ライ麦＋天然酵母で焼いており、店内キッチンで小麦粉を扱う=共用調理環境。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 卵・チーズ・バターを使うトーストやケーキが主力で、乳・卵と同一環境。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 『小麦アレルギーの人も楽しめるメニューを』と掲げ、米粉ワッフル・米粉キャロットケーキをグルテンフリーとして提供。

positives.text: 特別栽培米の米粉と有機米油を使った米粉キャロットケーキをグルテンフリーとして明示。

places.name: vegan restaurant cafuu (カフー)
places.gf_detail: cafuu is a fully vegan restaurant whose retail baked goods and bread are its gluten-free strong point: the venue states plainly "焼き菓子やパン販売：12時～(allグルテンフリー)" — all baked goods and bread sold from noon are gluten-free — and the pastry chef's Instagram bio confirms items are "卵・乳・白砂糖不使用、グルテンフリー". Those bakery items are the celiac-safe zone. The reservation-only omakase lunch course is a separate matter and must be confirmed dish-by-dish: as a vegan course it can rely on wheat-derived proteins (seitan / グルテンミート / 生麩 / 車麩) for mains, and the reported course historically included a wheat "白パン" (kodama-yeast white bread), so the lunch is NOT gluten-free. Kept at "options" for this reason — bakery counter safe, lunch needs item-by-item verification with the chef when reserving.
places.vegan_detail: Fully vegan (完全菜食). Vegewel and Hachidory both categorize cafuu as すべてVEGAN, the shop brands itself "vegan restaurant cafuu," and the owners note "cafuuは、作り手もヴィーガンです" (the makers are vegan too). Everything — the omakase lunch course and the counter bakery — is free of animal products, and dishes use ~50 varieties of seasonal organic, pesticide-free vegetables with completely additive-free (完全無添加) seasonings.
places.hours_raw: Lunch 10:30~ (reservation required, book ~2 days in advance; one group of 2-4 per day). Baked goods & bread sales 12:00~ (all gluten-free). Café 15:00~ (roughly to 17:00). Reservation-only, one group per day; closed days not clearly published — confirm by phone 079-227-6023.
places.neighborhood: 西新在家 (Nishi-Shinzaike), Himeji — ~3km west of Himeji Station
places.cuisine: Vegan omakase course lunch + gluten-free vegan bakery
places.notes: [GF] Bakery-safe, lunch-ask. The retail baked goods and bread (sold from 12:00) are the venue's own "allグルテンフリー" line and are the reliable celiac-safe choice for a 13-year-old. The reservation-only vegan omakase lunch is NOT gluten-free by default — vegan mains can use seitan/wheat gluten (グルテンミート・生麩・車麩) and the course historically included a wheat white bread — so if lunch is booked, tell them 'セリアック病・小麦アレルギー、グルテン厳禁' and confirm every dish item-by-item, or stick to the GF bakery counter. Lunch is one group of 2-4 per day and must be reserved ~2 days ahead (TEL 079-227-6023).

chef_bio.background: cafuu is run by the husband-and-wife team of chef Michiya Tsuri (釣満也, @cafuu3283) and pastry chef Mayuko Tsuri (釣真優子, @momomayu321). Before Himeji, the chef cooked in Okinawa — reportedly around six years on Ishigaki Island and several more elsewhere in Okinawa — and the shop's name 'cafuu' (カフー) carries the Okinawan sense of good fortune / a treasured blessing. The couple opened cafuu in Himeji in 2014 and relocated from the former 西今宿 location to the current 西新在家 1-1-8 address in February 2020. The kitchen is built around roughly 50 kinds of seasonal, pesticide-free vegetables from local farmers, prepared additive-free; the wife runs the gluten-free, egg/dairy/white-sugar-free vegan bakery counter.
chef_bio.philosophy: To prove that『ヴィーガン＝美味しい』(vegan equals delicious) using organic, pesticide-free, completely additive-free (完全無添加) ingredients — the makers are vegan themselves.
chef_bio.specialty: Reservation-only vegan omakase course lunch; gluten-free vegan baked goods and bread sold by weight from the counter.

anecdotes.text: The makers describe themselves as vegan too — "cafuuは、作り手もヴィーガンです" — and ask guests to bring their own containers and water bottles and to avoid scented detergents/fabric softeners, reflecting an eco/low-impact ethos.
Lunch is a single omakase course for just one group of 2-4 people per day, by reservation only.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Vegewel lists cafuu as a Himeji vegan restaurant at 西新在家1-1-8 with all-gluten-free baked goods/bread from noon and a reservation-required lunch. Hachidory categorizes it すべてVEGAN, run by a chef-and-pastry-chef couple, one group per day. Tekuteku Himeji documents the Feb 2020 relocation and the couple's Okinawa background. Instagram @cafuu3283 (chef Michiya Tsuri) and @momomayu321 (pastry chef Mayuko Tsuri) confirm the vegan, additive-free, gluten-free bakery identity.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Venue explicitly markets its counter baked goods and bread as "allグルテンフリー," but it is not a dedicated gluten-free kitchen: the same premises also prepares the omakase lunch, which can contain wheat (seitan-type vegan proteins and a wheat white bread), so shared-surface cross-contact for the lunch is plausible and unconfirmed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No published statement on whether soy sauce used in the vegan lunch is wheat-based shoyu or tamari; standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat, so this must be asked when reserving.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fully vegan kitchen (完全菜食) — no animal products used anywhere, so vegan cross-contact is not a concern.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Owners are vegan and run an additive-free, allergen-conscious bakery (egg/dairy/white-sugar-free, gluten-free), suggesting willingness to discuss diet, but no specific celiac/gluten-allergy protocol is documented; the reservation-only, one-group-per-day lunch format allows dish-by-dish confirmation in advance by phone.

positives.text: Retail baked goods and bread are the venue's own declared "allグルテンフリー" line — a reliable GF choice for a celiac.
Fully vegan, completely additive-free kitchen using ~50 seasonal pesticide-free vegetables; small one-group-per-day format enables personalized advance requests.

places.name: Miel Yakigashi Koubou (Miel焼菓子工房)
places.gf_detail: A dedicated allergy-response baked-goods workshop whose own site states 「当店のお菓子は全てグルテンフリーです」 (all our confections are gluten-free) and 「卵・乳製品・小麦不使用」 (no eggs, dairy, or wheat used). Critically for celiac safety, the workshop pledges 「卵・乳製品・小麦を持ち込まない」 — eggs, dairy, and wheat are never brought into the facility — so there is no wheat flour on the premises to cross-contaminate. Base ingredients are 米粉・豆乳・砂糖・なたね油・アーモンド and aluminum-free baking powder; no soy sauce, miso, or barley products are involved. This is a genuinely dedicated wheat-free kitchen; the only residual note is that almonds and soy (soy milk) are used, so nut/soy-allergic diners should consult, but for a celiac this is among the safest possible options.
places.vegan_detail: Products contain no egg, no dairy, and no butter — the base is rice flour, soy milk, sugar, rapeseed (canola) oil, and almonds — so the great majority of the baked goods are effectively vegan (e.g. butter-free madeleines, baked donuts, cookies, scones, silky soy-milk pudding). The shop name 'Miel' means honey in French, and honey appears in some allergy-friendly bakeries, so a strict vegan should confirm per item whether 蜂蜜 is used; sugar (砂糖) is listed as the standard sweetener. No explicit 'vegan' certification is claimed.
places.hours_raw: 10:00–17:30, closed Mon & Sun
places.neighborhood: 飾磨区加茂 (Kamo, Shikama-ku), Himeji — near Sanyo Electric Railway Nishi-Shikama Station (~10 min walk)
places.cuisine: Gluten-free / allergy-response baked goods & cakes (rice-flour patisserie)
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A dedicated wheat-, egg-, and dairy-free rice-flour bakery — wheat is never brought onto the premises, making it exceptionally safe for a celiac child. Everything in the case (cakes, scones, cookies, madeleines, focaccia, rice-flour bread) is gluten-free, so kids can freely point and choose. It is a takeaway workshop, not a café, and the shop's own domain is intermittently offline; hours are Tue–Sat 10:00–17:30, closed Sun & Mon, so verify by phone (079-233-5274) before visiting.

chef_bio.background: Miel焼菓子工房 is the baked-goods division of ルミエール合同会社 (Lumiere LLC), based at 672-8074 兵庫県姫路市飾磨区加茂339-8, which also runs learning-support and construction businesses. The bakery was founded out of an allergy-motivated mission: the company describes children who could not share the same cake as everyone else because of allergies, and states 「アレルギーがあるからと皆と一緒のケーキが食べられない。そんな思いをしているお子様の笑顔のため、安心してお召し上がりいただけるお菓子を作っております」. The workshop is housed in a charming old wooden building and specializes in cakes, panna cotta, butter-free madeleines, baked donuts, cookies, and rice-flour bread, all made without eggs, dairy, or wheat. Staff conduct allergy intake interviews and will make custom items (including soy-free) on consultation. The named individual owner/representative is not published on the sources reviewed.
chef_bio.philosophy: Make treats that allergy-affected children can eat safely and share with everyone, so no child is left out — 卵・乳製品・小麦を持ち込まない dedicated production for genuine peace of mind.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour (米粉) gluten-free cakes and baked goods; 1st-birthday allergy-safe cakes; silky soy-milk pudding.

anecdotes.text: The shop conducts allergy intake interviews and will create custom products, including soy-free options, on consultation.
Founded so that children who couldn't eat the same cake as everyone due to allergies could smile and share treats safely.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: sweetsguide.jp と lumierellc.net によれば、Miel焼菓子工房はルミエール合同会社が運営するアレルギー対応の焼菓子工房で、卵・乳製品・小麦を持ち込まず、米粉・豆乳・砂糖・なたね油・アーモンド・アルミフリーBPで製造。アレルギーの子どもが皆と同じおやつを食べられるようにという想いから創業。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 「卵・乳製品・小麦を持ち込まない」 — eggs, dairy, and wheat are never brought into the workshop, eliminating wheat-flour cross-contact at source.
「当店のお菓子は全てグルテンフリーです」 — every product in the shop is gluten-free.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Sweets bakery — no soy sauce used; base ingredients are rice flour, soy milk, sugar, rapeseed oil, almonds, aluminum-free baking powder.

vegan_cross_contact.text: No egg, dairy, or butter used anywhere; soy milk and rapeseed oil replace animal products. Honey status per-item unconfirmed given the shop name 'Miel'.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Staff conduct allergy intake interviews (アレルギーの聞き取り) and make custom products, including soy-free, on consultation.

positives.text: Founded specifically to serve children with food allergies so they can eat safely alongside everyone.
Dedicated wheat-free facility with an explicit no-wheat-on-premises policy.

places.name: Miinas (ミーナーズ)
places.gf_detail: Miinas brands itself as a グルテンフリー・オーガニックカフェ (gluten-free organic cafe); owner Minami Kenmi told Himeji Keizai Shimbun the food is 「オーガニック食材やグルテンフリーを意識した内容」 (content mindful of organic ingredients and gluten-free), aiming to 「オーガニック食やグルテンフリーを日常の選択肢として広げたい」. The signature bagel burger is made with 国産有機米粉100% (100% domestic organic rice flour) per the owner-interviewed keizai.biz and the budou-chan seed, and morning plates use 酵素玄米おにぎり (enzyme brown-rice onigiri) and 有機米粉ベーグル (organic rice-flour bagels), so the food menu reads as wholly rice/rice-flour based. However there is NO explicit celiac cross-contact or 小麦不使用専用 (dedicated wheat-free) protocol stated, the owner is a wheat-trained (Germany/France) pastry chef, and Hakodate Pilsner (barley) beer is served on premises — so treat as fully-GF food but not certified celiac-safe. One local blog (himeji-spice.net) conflictingly describes the bagel as 国産小麦100% (wheat); this contradicts the owner-sourced keizai.biz and the venue's own GF branding and is likely a transcription error, but confirm at the counter for a celiac child.
places.vegan_detail: Not marketed as vegan; menu centers on egg/dairy bakes (rice-flour pancakes, roll cake) and meat/chicken plates (teriyaki, salad chicken, karaage wraps). Reliably plant-based choices are limited to black hot coffee and apple juice, with the enzyme drink and enzyme brown-rice onigiri plate likely vegan-adaptable but unconfirmed. No sourced vegan-labelled meal exists; ask staff about a plain onigiri or salad plate.
places.hours_raw: 9:00–18:00 (L.O. 17:30); Thursday closed. Morning service 9:00–11:00, lunch from 11:00. (Trimming salon closed Wed & Thu.)
places.neighborhood: 飾西 (Shikisai), Himeji — 60m from JR 余部駅 (Yobe Station), Bantan Line
places.cuisine: Gluten-free organic cafe — rice-flour bagels, rice burgers, wraps, plates & sweets
places.notes: [GF] Rice-flour-based gluten-free organic cafe that opened 2025-10-01, 60m from JR Yobe Station. Food menu (rice-flour bagels/bagel burger, enzyme brown-rice onigiri, rice-flour pancakes) reads as fully gluten-free, but there is no dedicated celiac/cross-contact protocol on record — the owner is a wheat-trained pastry chef and barley beer is poured on premises, so confirm no shared surfaces before ordering for a celiac. Two specific traps: the 照り焼き (teriyaki) wrap likely uses wheat-containing soy sauce, and the 唐揚げ (karaage) coating is unconfirmed as rice-flour — ask about both. The co-located trimming salon is walled off from the cafe and handles no food.

chef_bio.background: Miinas is owned by Minami Kenmi (見満みなみ), who graduated from a confectionery/pâtisserie school in 2012 and then spent roughly eight years studying pastry and cooking in Germany and France (Himeji Keizai Shimbun). She opened Miinas on 2025-10-01 in the North Aoyama building beside JR Yobe Station, pairing a gluten-free × organic × additive-free cafe with a dog trimming salon (run by groomer Rie Miyama, 深山梨江) walled off in the same building. The cafe seats 16 across 6 tables in an approximately 31-tsubo space and offers all items for takeout.
chef_bio.philosophy: To spread organic food and gluten-free eating as everyday choices — 「オーガニック食やグルテンフリーを日常の選択肢として広げたい」 — with 体にやさしい (body-gentle) cooking.
chef_bio.specialty: 100% domestic organic rice-flour (国産有機米粉100%) bagels and bagel burgers, rice-flour pancakes, and enzyme brown-rice onigiri plates.

anecdotes.text: Trained in pastry and cooking in Germany and France for about eight years before returning to open her own gluten-free organic cafe in her home region.
The cafe shares a building with a reservation-only dog trimming salon, with the two spaces separated by a wall so each stays comfortable.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 姫路経済新聞: オーナー見満みなみは2012年に製菓学校を卒業後、ドイツ・フランスで約8年間製菓と料理を学び、2025年10月1日に姫路市飾西にグルテンフリー×オーガニックのカフェ『Miinas』をオープン。国産有機米粉100%のベーグルバーガーなどを提供し、「オーガニック食やグルテンフリーを日常の選択肢として広げたい」と話す。トリミングサロンを併設し壁で仕切っている。

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated-fryer or celiac cross-contact protocol is stated on any source; the karaage wrap implies frying, and shared oil/surfaces cannot be ruled out — confirm on site.
Owner is a wheat-trained (Germany/France) pastry chef and Hakodate Pilsner (barley) beer is poured on premises, so gluten is present in the building even if the food menu is rice-flour based.
One local blog describes the bagel as 国産小麦100% (100% domestic wheat), conflicting with the owner-sourced keizai.biz 国産有機米粉100% (rice flour) — verify the bagel flour in person.
Co-located dog trimming salon is walled off from the cafe and involves no food, so it does not add wheat cross-contact to meals.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The 照り焼き (teriyaki) wrap option almost certainly uses wheat-containing soy sauce unless tamari is used — unconfirmed; ask before ordering.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Not a vegan kitchen; dairy, egg and meat handled alongside plant items. No sourced vegan cross-contact information.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No sourced description of allergy handling or celiac accommodation beyond the general gluten-free-conscious concept.

positives.text: Self-branded gluten-free × organic × additive-free cafe with a food menu built on 国産有機米粉100% rice flour and enzyme brown rice — inherently wheat-free ingredients.
Owner personally trained in patisserie/cooking abroad for ~8 years and set the cafe's stated goal of making gluten-free an everyday choice.

places.name: MIL bake (米粉専門店ミルベイク)
places.gf_detail: A dedicated rice-flour (米粉専門店) home workshop in Himeji; the owner states plainly 「工房では小麦粉を使用しません」 ("we do not use wheat flour in the workshop") and 「国産米粉となるべく身体に負担のない材料で」. All breads are described as 米粉100% and are 「小麦・乳・卵・ナッツ不使用」 (no wheat, dairy, egg or nuts). Because the entire solo workshop is wheat-free — the owner is herself a former pâtissier who developed an occupational wheat allergy — cross-contact risk from flour is minimal; nuts and soy are handled on site, so nut/soy-allergic guests should note that, but for celiac this is as safe as it gets in Harima.
places.vegan_detail: The core rice-flour breads (山食パン, まるパン, バケット, 豆乳ロールパン) use only 国産米粉・国産片栗粉・米油・てんさい糖・天日塩・ドライイースト・オオバコ and are explicitly 「小麦・乳・卵・ナッツ不使用」, so they are vegan. However the baked sweets (financiers, cookies, florentines) and all decoration cakes/tarts contain egg and dairy, and several savoury breads (塩バターパン, ウィンナーロール, 厚焼き玉子サンド) contain butter, sausage or egg — so the shop is not fully vegan.
places.hours_raw: Irregular — check open days on the official Instagram (sells out and closes when products run out). Bread days ~11:00–15:00; cake pickup days ~12:00–15:00. Solo operator; days vary week to week.
places.neighborhood: 姫路市田寺 (Tadera, ~2.5 km west of Himeji Castle), Hyogo
places.cuisine: Rice-flour (komeko) bakery — bread, baked sweets & celebration cakes
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A solo, wheat-free rice-flour workshop — the owner uses no wheat flour on the premises, so celiac cross-contact from flour is essentially nil. Breads are 米粉100% and vegan-friendly; sweets and cakes contain egg/dairy but are still gluten-free. Hours are irregular and stock sells out, so check the Instagram (@mil.bakeshop) and reserve/DM before travelling; cash only, ~2.5 km west of Himeji Castle with only 2 parking spaces.

chef_bio.background: MIL bake is run single-handedly by Miyuki, a trained pâtissier who developed an occupational wheat allergy while working with flour — she reports asthma, rhinitis, dermatitis and itching eyes on contact with wheat. Forced away from conventional patisserie, she discovered rice flour (米粉) and reinvented her craft around it, opening MIL bake as a wheat-free workshop. The shop relocated and reopened at its current 田寺 (Tadera) address on 28 September 2025, having previously operated from a smaller home workshop with weekly Instagram-announced sales. She runs everything herself, taking orders via LINE and Instagram DM and warning that replies may be slow.
chef_bio.philosophy: 「国産米粉となるべく身体に負担のない材料で」— to bake with domestic rice flour and ingredients that are as gentle on the body as possible, and to make gluten-free food that everyone, allergic or not, finds genuinely delicious so that 米粉 products become a normal sight on shelves.
chef_bio.specialty: 米粉100% breads (山食パン loaf, baguette, rolls) plus rice-flour baked sweets and made-to-order decoration cakes and tarts.

anecdotes.text: As wheat allergies increase, the owner says she wants to offer 「グルテンフリーでも誰もが楽しめる美味しい食べ物」 — gluten-free food everyone can enjoy — not just something for the allergic.
The shop states it was created for 「小麦アレルギー、小麦不耐性、小麦をドクターストップされている方々」 — people with wheat allergy, wheat intolerance, or under doctor's orders to avoid wheat.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 公式サイト(crayonsite)、local-prime、tanosu、budou-chan の記事いずれも、店主が元パティシエで小麦アレルギーを発症し、米粉専門の工房を開いたと記載。「工房では小麦粉を使用しません」「米粉100%」「小麦・乳・卵・ナッツ不使用(パン)」を明言している。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 「工房では小麦粉を使用しません」— no wheat flour is used anywhere in the workshop, so flour cross-contact is not a factor.
All bread is described as 米粉100%; breads are 「小麦・乳・卵・ナッツ不使用」 per the shop.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Bakery — no soy sauce in products. 豆乳ロールパン contains soy milk (not soy sauce); soy-sensitive guests should note it.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Egg and dairy are used in the same wheat-free workshop for sweets and cakes; vegans should stick to the plain 米粉 breads listed as egg/dairy-free.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Owner is herself a wheat-allergic former pâtissier who built the shop specifically for wheat allergy / intolerance / medically wheat-restricted customers; fruit allergies and preferences accommodated on request.

positives.text: Entirely wheat-free rice-flour workshop run by an allergy-affected owner — 「なるべく身体に負担のない材料で」, using domestic rice flour, potato starch, rice oil, beet sugar and sea salt.
Breads free of wheat, dairy, egg and nuts — usable by celiac and vegan guests alike.

places.name: Reborn Curry (リボーンカレー)
places.gf_detail: This spice-curry specialist markets every curry as wheat-free: the official site says 「グレインフリーのカレーをご堪能下さい」(enjoy our grain-free curry) and the KissPress review confirms 「同店のカレーは全てグルテンフリー」(all of the shop's curries are gluten-free), also noting the curries are butter-free (バター不使用). The base is rice with a house blend of 15 spices, so the curry plates themselves should contain no wheat. HOWEVER this is a SHARED (not dedicated) kitchen and the menu also includes samosa / keema-samosa (サモサ / キーマサモサ) whose pastry is traditionally wheat flour, so wheat is handled on premises — cross-contact is possible and a celiac should stick to the curry plates, skip the samosa, and confirm the day's spice blend and any garnish contain no wheat.
places.vegan_detail: The mains are all animal-based — bone-in chicken curries, meat keema, and boiled egg — and the lassi and cheesecake are dairy, so there is no vegan main. A few plant sides exist (野菜のスパイス炒め vegetable spice stir-fry, スパイスポテト spiced potatoes, アチャール pickles) and black coffee, which may be vegan but are not confirmed as animal-free by the shop. A vegan diner would need to ask and cobble together sides.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Tue & Fri–Sun 11:00–15:00 and 17:00–21:00 (curry L.O. 14:30 / 20:30, café 15:00–17:00); Thu 11:00–15:00 lunch only; closed Wednesdays
places.neighborhood: 岡田 (Okada), 手柄駅 area, Himeji
places.notes: [GF] Spice-curry specialist that brands all of its curries as gluten-free / grain-free and butter-free — a strong fit for a celiac, and there is a kids' curry (キッズカレー) making it workable for a 13-year-old. This is NOT a dedicated GF kitchen: samosa (wheat pastry) is on the menu, so avoid it and ask staff to keep the curry plate away from any floured items. Rice-based plates with a 15-spice house blend; confirm the blend and any garnishes are wheat-free on the day. Closed Wednesdays; Thursday is lunch only.

chef_bio.chef_name: 森石 (Moriishi / reported elsewhere as Morizaki)
chef_bio.background: Reborn Curry opened on May 1, 2024 in the Okada district of Himeji, operated by GP&M, an amusement-equipment company for which this is a first venture into food service (per Himeji Keizai Shimbun). The manager (given as 森石 / Moriishi in KissPress and as Morizaki in the Keizai report) is described as passionate about both music and curry. He was inspired by an Indian/spice restaurant he frequented as a university student in Matsumoto, Nagano — a shop that has since closed — and set out to 'reborn' those flavors, which is the source of the shop's name. The venue doubles as a record café where guests can request vinyl.
chef_bio.philosophy: 「専門店のカレーとコーヒーで元気（パワー）を提供する」— to recharge customers' energy through specialty curry and coffee; healthy, wheat-free and butter-free cooking so the stomach stays light after eating.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-based spice curry using a 15-spice house blend — signature dry keema paired with a soupier chicken curry, plus 3-hour-simmered bone-in chicken curry.

anecdotes.text: The shop is named 'Reborn' because the owner set out to revive the flavors of a now-closed spice/Indian restaurant he loved as a student in Matsumoto, Nagano.
It is a record café: vinyl lines the walls and customers can request songs or bring their own records.
The operator, GP&M, is an amusement-equipment company entering food service for the first time.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: KissPress, Tanosu, Budou-chan and Himeji Keizai Shimbun all confirm a May 2024 opening in Okada, a 15-spice rice-based curry style, the record-café concept, and the manager's music-and-curry passion; KissPress and Budou-chan explicitly state the curries are gluten-free / grain-free and butter-free.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shared (not dedicated GF) kitchen; samosa / keema-samosa on the menu use traditionally wheat-flour pastry, so wheat is present on premises and cross-contact is possible — celiac guests should avoid the samosa and confirm plating.
Curries use a 15-spice house blend; commercial garam-masala / curry-powder blends are occasionally cut with wheat flour, so the specific blend should be confirmed wheat-free on the day.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce is mentioned in the curry recipes; South-Asian-style spice curry typically uses none, but this is not explicitly confirmed — ask whether any しょうゆ/soy sauce is used and, if so, whether it is tamari.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Mains are chicken/keema/egg based and desserts/lassi are dairy; plant sides are not confirmed animal-free, so vegans must ask.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The shop actively advertises its curries as gluten-free / grain-free and butter-free, indicating awareness of wheat-avoidance, but no formal allergen protocol or dedicated-kitchen claim is documented.

positives.text: Official site: 「グレインフリーのカレーをご堪能下さい」— enjoy our grain-free curry.
KissPress review: 「同店のカレーは全てグルテンフリー」— all of the shop's curries are gluten-free.
Budou-chan: curries are 【グルテンフリー】【バター不使用】(gluten-free, butter-free).
A kids' curry (キッズカレー) is offered, making the shop family/child-friendly.

places.name: Retreat Field (リトリートフィールド · 姫路こころの事業団)
places.gf_detail: Welfare-run farm (就労継続支援B型) in northern Himeji that direct-sells rice-flour snacks and noodles made from its own naturally-cultivated rice — 『自然栽培農法で育てたお米や、その米粉を使った米粉麺』. The rice-flour dried noodles are marketed as gluten-free so 『小麦アレルギーの人でも食べられる』, and the signature snack 『ぽりっと』 (potato + rice flour) won bronze at the 2022 授産品コンクール. These are single-ingredient-transparent rice products, but this is a small production/直売 facility, not a certified gluten-free line — confirm the specific product and that it is not made on a shared wheat line. Note it is primarily a shop/直売所; hot food only at the monthly はたけカフェ.
places.vegan_detail: The rice-flour snacks (ぽりっと) and dried rice noodles are plant-based; the seasonal プリン (pudding) likely contains egg/dairy. Vegan is item-by-item — confirm.
places.hours_raw: Farm-stand sales 10:00–17:00, closed Sat, Sun & holidays. The Hatake Cafe (lunch set) is held once a month. Online sales via BASE (makinorf.thebase.in). Parking available. 7 min by car from Funatsu IC.
places.neighborhood: 山田町牧野 (Yamada-chō Makino), 姫路市北部 Himeji (northern, away from centre)
places.cuisine: Rice-flour sweets & noodles direct-sales (welfare farm) + monthly farm cafe
places.gmaps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=リトリートフィールド%20山田町牧野%20姫路市%20Japan
places.notes: [GF] A direct-sales shop, not a restaurant — buy the rice-flour 米粉乾燥麺 and ぽりっと snack (naturally-cultivated rice, marketed GF) or order online via BASE. Weekends and holidays are closed; hot meals only at the once-a-month はたけカフェ. Confirm the production line is not shared with wheat before buying for a coeliac child.

chef_bio.background: Retreat Field is operated by NPO法人 姫路こころの事業団 as a Type-B continuous employment support workplace (就労継続支援B型事業所) in Yamada-chō Makino, northern Himeji. It grows rice and vegetables by natural cultivation (no pesticide/fertiliser) and develops rice-flour products so that people with wheat allergy can eat them. Its potato-and-rice-flour snack 『ぽりっと』 won a bronze prize at the 2022 はばたけ授産品コンクール, and it has developed chewy rice-flour dried noodles (reported at ¥290 per serving). Products are sold on-site and online via BASE, with a monthly はたけカフェ.
chef_bio.philosophy: Provide meaningful work in a natural-cultivation farm while making gluten-free rice-flour food that wheat-allergic and coeliac people can safely enjoy.
chef_bio.specialty: Naturally-cultivated rice, rice-flour dried noodles, ぽりっと potato-rice-flour snack, seasonal veg/fruit pudding.

anecdotes.text: 『ぽりっと』ははばたけ授産品コンクール2022で銅賞を受賞。米粉乾燥麺は1食290円で販売予定と報道。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 姫路みたい特集・神戸新聞・BASE通販より。NPO法人姫路こころの事業団が運営する就労継続支援B型の農園。自然栽培米の米粉を使ったグルテンフリーの菓子(ぽりっと)・米粉乾燥麺を直売/通販。月1回はたけカフェ開催。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 小規模な就労施設の製造・直売所。米粉製品専用ラインか小麦と共用かは未確認。要確認。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 季節のプリンは卵・乳を含む可能性。ぽりっと・米粉麺は植物性。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 『自然栽培農法で育てたお米や、その米粉を使った米粉麺』を販売。米粉麺は小麦アレルギーの人でも食べられるとする。

positives.text: 自然栽培米100%の米粉を使ったグルテンフリーの菓子・乾燥麺。ぽりっとは授産品コンクール銅賞。

places.name: SAI CAFE (サイカフェ)
places.gf_detail: Fully vegan cafe on the north side of Himeji Castle. The packaged baked sweets (produced by 岡山『いぶき』/Ibuki) are the confirmed gluten-free line: donuts, cookies, madeleines, lemon cake and snowballs, all 『動物性原材料不使用且つ8大アレルゲンフリー』(no animal ingredients and free of the 8 major allergens — which includes wheat), made with rice flour instead of wheat. CAVEAT: the signature 斎麺 (sai-men vegetable ramen: ginger / ashitaba / chlorella noodles) has NO published gluten-free claim and ramen noodles are normally wheat — treat hot food as not verified GF. Craft beer (barley) is also served on premises. Stick to the 8-allergen-free Ibuki sweets and confirm any savoury dish directly.
places.vegan_detail: Entirely plant-based. The shop states its menu uses 『動物性原材料、精製糖、うまみ調味料、アルコール調味料、乳製品は使わない』 (no animal ingredients, refined sugar, MSG, alcohol seasonings or dairy). Recipes are supervised by Tokyo vegan restaurant 菜道 (Saido), rated among the world's best. Vegan beer available too.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–17:00 (food L.O. 16:00 / drink & sweets L.O. 16:30). Temporarily closed from around December 2025 for a menu renewal, reopening planned for spring 2026. The official site posts normal business hours. Before visiting, confirm the operating status on the official Instagram (@saicafe__himeji).
places.neighborhood: 城北新町 (Jōhoku-shinmachi), 姫路城北側 Himeji
places.cuisine: Vegan cafe & restaurant (vegetable ramen, curry, sweets, specialty coffee)
places.notes: [GF] Vegan cafe; confirmed GF items are the Ibuki packaged sweets (8-major-allergen-free, rice flour). The house 斎麺 ramen noodles carry no GF claim and are presumed wheat, and barley craft beer is served, so this is an options venue for a coeliac, not a safe hot-meal choice. IMPORTANT: was temporarily closed for a menu renewal until spring 2026 — verify it has reopened via Instagram before travelling.

chef_bio.background: Owner Yano Eitarō previously ran an izakaya in Himeji. He recognised that Himeji Castle draws many international visitors yet the city lacked vegetarian/vegan dining, and after encountering chef Kusumoto Katsushige (楠本勝三) of Tokyo vegan restaurant 菜道 (Saido) he opened SAI CAFE on 21 March 2023. The recipes are supervised by Saido, which holds a 'world's best vegan restaurant' reputation. The name 『SAI』 layers 菜 (vegetables), the signature 斎麺, 菜道 (the mentoring restaurant) and サイ (rhinoceros).
chef_bio.philosophy: Deliver 'junkie-satisfying' yet fully plant-based food — no animal products, refined sugar, MSG, alcohol seasoning or dairy — so health-conscious and international diners can enjoy it.
chef_bio.specialty: 斎麺 (vegetable ramen with ginger/ashitaba/chlorella noodles), vegetable curry, vegan sweets and specialty coffee.

anecdotes.text: 菜道シェフ楠本勝三氏との出会いがきっかけで開業、レシピは菜道が監修。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 姫路経済新聞・Kiss PRESS・Food Diversity・公式サイトより。元居酒屋店主の矢野英太郎氏が2023年3月に開業。東京の菜道が監修する完全ヴィーガン店。菓子は岡山いぶき製で動物性不使用＋8大アレルゲンフリー(米粉使用)。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 斎麺(ラーメン)の麺にグルテンフリー表記なし=通常小麦麺の想定。焼き菓子(いぶき製)は8大アレルゲンフリーだが提供環境は要確認。
店内でクラフトビール(Free Spirits Brewing／大麦)を提供。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 完全ヴィーガン。動物性原材料・乳製品・精製糖・うまみ調味料・アルコール調味料は不使用。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 『動物性原材料不使用且つ8大アレルゲンフリーだけでなく、着色料や添加物も不使用で安心・安全の商品』としてヴィーガン菓子を販売。

positives.text: ドーナツ・クッキー・マドレーヌ・レモンケーキ・スノーボールは動物性不使用＋8大アレルゲンフリー(小麦不使用・米粉)。

places.name: Organic Cafe Restaurant Sangohachi (珊瑚椀)
places.gf_detail: Organic natural-food cafe/restaurant one minute from JR Gochaku station. The kitchen uses homemade organic koji (麹) seasonings and states 『調理にはなるべく小麦粉や砂糖を使っていない』 (they avoid using wheat flour and sugar as much as possible), so many plates are flour-free — e.g. the バターナッツかぼちゃのハンバーグ is made 『小麦粉を使わず』 (without wheat flour). BUT wheat is not eliminated: 小麦粉は一部メニューのみ使用 (wheat is used in some items), toast is on the menu, and notably the signature pound cake is 『九州産の自然栽培の大麦粉を100%使用』 (100% barley flour = gluten). This is a negotiable, ask-first venue: request a wheat-free plate and confirm the soy sauce/miso used with the koji seasonings. Not a coeliac-dedicated kitchen.
places.vegan_detail: Offers explicit vegan choices such as the バターナッツかぼちゃのハンバーグ弁当 (labelled 'for vegan'). Uses organic eggs and dairy elsewhere, so vegan is item-by-item — ask.
places.hours_raw: Tue–Fri 11:30–22:00 (L.O. 21:00) / Sat 9:00–22:00 (L.O. 21:00) / Sun & holidays 9:00–17:00 (L.O. 16:00). Morning menu by reservation only, Sat/Sun/holidays 9:00–10:30. Closed Mon (if a holiday, open 9:00–17:00 and closed the following Tue).
places.neighborhood: 御国野町御着 (Mikunino-chō Gochaku), 姫路市 Himeji
places.cuisine: Organic natural-food cafe & restaurant (koji seasonings, 'organic junkie')
places.gmaps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=珊瑚椀%20御国野町御着%20姫路市%20Japan
places.notes: [GF] Ask-first. Much of the cooking is deliberately wheat-flour-free and built on homemade koji, and staff can prepare a flour-free plate — but wheat is still used in some dishes, toast is served, and the house pound cake is 100% barley flour (gluten). For a coeliac child, phone ahead, explain セリアック, ask for a 小麦粉不使用 plate and confirm the soy sauce/miso is wheat-free (barley miso is common).

chef_bio.background: Owner Kaizuka Akiko developed an interest in organic food as a student after struggling with atopy, chemical sensitivity and hay fever, which led her to limit everything she puts into her body to organic and additive-free ingredients. She opened 珊瑚椀 around 2019 near JR Gochaku station, marking a third anniversary in 2022, and built the concept around homemade organic koji seasonings — dubbing the style 『オーガニックジャンキー』 (organic junk food that is nonetheless clean). All ingredients are organic/pesticide-free with no additives or chemical seasonings.
chef_bio.philosophy: Serve satisfying 'organic junkie' comfort food using only organic, additive-free ingredients and homemade koji seasonings; minimise wheat flour and sugar.
chef_bio.specialty: Weekly-changing organic lunch, koji-seasoned dishes, butternut-squash koji hamburg (flour-free, vegan).

anecdotes.text: アトピー・化学物質過敏症・花粉症の経験から、体に入れる食材・調味料を有機・無添加に限定した店を構えた。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 姫路経済新聞(3周年記事)・TANOSU取材・公式サイトより。店主貝塚暁子さんが2019年頃開業。自家製麹調味料＋有機食材の『オーガニックジャンキー』。小麦粉・砂糖はなるべく不使用だが一部メニューで小麦使用、パウンドケーキは大麦粉100%。

gf_cross_contamination.text: 小麦粉は一部メニューのみ使用、トーストも提供＝共用調理環境。要相談。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 自家製麹調味料は有機だが、醤油・味噌の小麦・大麦有無は未確認。要確認。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 有機卵・乳を使うメニューもあり、ヴィーガンは要指定。

staff_allergy_handling.text: 『調理にはなるべく小麦粉や砂糖を使っていない』。バターナッツかぼちゃのハンバーグは小麦粉不使用で作れる。

positives.text: 有機・無添加・無農薬食材のみ使用、自家製麹調味料はグルテンフリー。多くの料理が小麦粉不使用。

places.name: Motomachi Satonaka Himeji (元町サトナカ 姫路店)
places.gf_detail: The venue's own words: 「無添加・無化調・小麦粉不使用グルテンフリーのスパイスチキンカレー」 and 「すべてのカレーが小麦粉不使用なのでアレルギー持ちの人も安心」 — every curry sauce is made with no wheat flour, thickened by an 8-spice blend and an additive-free dashi of kombu, shiitake and chicken bones simmered daily, served over turmeric rice. However this is a SHARED (not dedicated) kitchen that also cooks wheat: the chicken katsu curry and the chicken-cutlet topping are panko-breaded and fried, and the Himeji branch also serves keema curry pasta and cheese curry doria. Because breaded/fried wheat items and pasta are prepared in the same space, the wheat-free curries are best treated as GF options with real cross-contact risk rather than a fully gluten-free venue — order a plain curry (no katsu, no seasoned egg), confirm the topping and any seasoning, and flag the celiac requirement.
places.vegan_detail: There is no vegan main: the curry dashi is built on chicken bones (鶏ガラ) and the curries contain chicken. Only side/topping items lean plant-based — the cabbage pickles (ピクルス ¥100) and the potato sabzi that accompanies the keema are vegetable-based, while the lassi and cheese are dairy. A vegan diner could not make a meal here beyond sides.
places.hours_raw: Mon–Sat 11:30–15:00 (L.O. 14:30) & 17:30–20:00 (L.O. 19:30); Sun 11:30–15:00 lunch only; closed 1st & 3rd Mondays
places.neighborhood: 安田町 (Yasuda-cho), near Tegara Station / south of JR Himeji Station, Himeji
places.notes: [GF] Every curry sauce here is genuinely made without wheat flour — the shop advertises 「小麦粉不使用グルテンフリーのスパイスチキンカレー」 and it is a good, mild, kid-friendly curry-and-rice (turmeric rice) for a celiac 13-year-old. But it is a shared kitchen that also fries panko chicken katsu and cooks pasta and doria, so avoid the katsu curry, the chicken-cutlet topping and the seasoned soft-boiled egg (soy-based marinade), order a plain chicken or mushroom-chicken curry, and clearly state the wheat allergy so staff plate it away from the fried/breaded items.

chef_bio.background: Motomachi Satonaka is a noren-wake (independent spin-off) of the original 'Satonaka' curry shop in Sannomiya, Kobe. The Motomachi flagship opened in 2014 about five minutes north of JR Motomachi Station, after its owner tasted the original Satonaka curry, was moved to declare 「このカレーをつくりたい！」 ('I want to make this curry!'), and trained until he could reproduce the recipe 'down to the gram.' The Himeji branch opened on 19 February 2023 as the group's second location, run by a store manager named Ichii who trained at the Motomachi shop. The brand has since expanded to Takasago and Suzurandai branches as well.
chef_bio.philosophy: Additive-free and no chemical seasonings ('無添加・無化調'), gluten-free and 'body-friendly' (からだにやさしい) — wheat flour is never used to thicken the curry; umami comes instead from a slow dashi of kombu, shiitake and chicken bones simmered about three hours daily.
chef_bio.specialty: Wheat-free spice chicken curry built on an 8-spice blend and chicken-bone/kombu/shiitake dashi, served over turmeric rice; domestic chicken breast is defatted and cooked separately at low temperature.

anecdotes.text: The owner was so struck by the original Sannomiya Satonaka curry that he resolved 「このカレーをつくりたい！」 and trained to master the recipe by the gram.
The Himeji branch's store manager, Ichii, trained at the original Motomachi location before opening the Himeji shop on 19 February 2023.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: Japanese sources (tanosu, kisspress, the brand's own site) consistently describe Satonaka as an additive-free, no-chemical-seasoning, gluten-free spice-curry shop whose curries use no wheat flour; they attribute the shop to an owner who spun off from the Sannomiya original in 2014 and give the Himeji manager's name as Ichii, but no single named founder/chef and no soy-sauce or vegan detail is published.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shared kitchen: the chicken katsu curry and chicken-cutlet topping are panko-breaded and fried, and the Himeji branch also serves keema curry pasta and cheese curry doria — all wheat items cooked in the same space as the wheat-free curries, so cross-contact is possible.
The shop's claim is specifically that the curries use no wheat flour ('すべてのカレーが小麦粉不使用'), not that the whole kitchen is gluten-free; no dedicated GF prep line or fryer is mentioned in any source.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No Japanese source mentions soy sauce (醤油) or tamari in the curry; the base is described as an additive-free dashi of kombu, shiitake and chicken bones, so the curry sauce itself appears free of soy sauce.
Caveat: the 半熟味付玉子 (seasoned soft-boiled egg) topping is a marinated egg that in Japan is typically steeped in a soy-sauce-based liquid (soy sauce usually contains wheat) — confirm before adding it.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Every curry contains chicken and a chicken-bone dashi, so vegan diners are limited to vegetable sides (pickles, potato sabzi); no vegan curry exists.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The shop markets its curry to allergy sufferers ('アレルギー持ちの人も安心'), indicating allergy awareness, but no source describes a specific cross-contact protocol for the shared kitchen.

positives.text: Every curry sauce is genuinely made without wheat flour ('無添加・無化調・小麦粉不使用グルテンフリーのスパイスチキンカレー'); thickening comes from an 8-spice blend and dashi, not roux.
Additive-free, no chemical seasonings, with a mild kid-friendly chicken curry over turmeric rice — a workable safe meal for a celiac child if ordered plain.

places.name: Saka no Ue no Ureshinaru (坂の上の嬉しなる)
places.gf_detail: Allergy-friendly wagashi/Western sweets cafe (opened June 2018). Its house cake sponges are all rice flour with 『卵・乳・小麦不使用』 (no egg, dairy or wheat), and made-to-order allergy cakes are offered, so genuine rice-flour gluten-free cakes exist here. CAVEAT: it is a made-to-order shop with 'anything-goes' fillings (『中身も変幻自在』) where グルテンフリー is just one of many options, and NOT every item is wheat-free — e.g. one signature cake is 『くるみ醤油と多良間黒糖 生麩仕立て』, and 生麩 (nama-fu) is wheat gluten and くるみ醤油 is wheat soy sauce. Confirm each specific cake, and note this is a small non-dedicated kitchen (cross-contact possible). For a coeliac, order only the explicitly 米粉/グルテンフリー cakes and say it is for a child.
places.vegan_detail: The allergy line is 卵・乳・小麦不使用 (no egg/dairy/wheat) so several cakes are effectively vegan, but other variations use 生クリーム (dairy cream) and chocolate. Vegan is item-by-item — confirm.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–17:00 (some sources say Fri until 16:30). Closed Tue & Sun (per Hitosara). Note: business days/hours differ between sources — confirm by phone. Made-to-order cakes require reservation.
places.neighborhood: 飾東町豊国 (Shikitō-chō Toyokuni), 姫路市 Himeji
places.gmaps: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=坂の上の嬉しなる%20飾東町豊国%20姫路市%20Japan
places.notes: [GF] Rice-flour sponge sweets shop with real gluten-free cakes, but it is made-to-order and not everything is wheat-free (one signature cake uses 生麩/wheat gluten and walnut soy sauce). Treat as options: order only the labelled 米粉/グルテンフリー cakes, ask about the specific filling, and note the small shared kitchen. Best to phone ahead (079-229-2277) and ask for a coeliac-safe custom cake.

chef_bio.background: Owner Nagai opened 坂の上の嬉しなる in June 2018, renovating a storage building on the family home into an allergy-friendly cake shop and small cafe. The shop name 『嬉しなる』 incorporates a character from his mother's name, and he roasts his own hojicha as a tribute to childhood memories of his mother making hojicha-yu (roasted-tea porridge). All sponge cakes use rice flour and are made egg-, dairy- and wheat-free, and he creates fully made-to-order character/allergy cakes.
chef_bio.philosophy: Make sweets that families with wheat, egg and dairy allergies can eat together and smile over ('笑顔がこぼれる'), combining Japanese ingredients (koji, miso, hojicha, amazake) with rice-flour sponge.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour allergy cakes, koji/hojicha wagashi-Western hybrids, made-to-order character cakes.

anecdotes.text: 自宅の倉庫を改装して2018年6月にアレルギー対応ケーキ屋を開業。母への思い出からほうじ茶を自家焙煎。

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: TANOSU取材・ヒトサラ・姫路みたいより。2018年6月開業のアレルギー対応ケーキ＆カフェ。スポンジは全て米粉・卵乳小麦不使用。オーダーメイドケーキ対応。ただし生麩仕立て等、一部に小麦を使うメニューもある。

gf_cross_contamination.text: オーダーメイドで中身は変幻自在。グルテンフリーは選択肢の一つで、生麩仕立て等小麦を使うケーキもある小規模共用キッチン。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 『くるみ醤油』を使う和スイーツあり=小麦醤油の可能性。要確認。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 生クリーム・チョコの組合せもあり、卵乳との共用環境。

staff_allergy_handling.text: スポンジは全て米粉で卵・乳・小麦不使用、アレルギー対応ケーキを提供。オーダーメイド可。

positives.text: 米粉スポンジのグルテンフリーケーキを常設・オーダーメイド対応。
